title: A Skunk Named Ava Escaped in Scotland. Nobody's Been Sprayed Yet.
script: A skunk loose in Scotland! On June 9, drivers on the B914 road near Kelty, Fife spotted something that almost never happens there: a skunk. Skunks are native to North America — they simply don't exist in the Scottish wild. This one, named Ava, is 5 years old and burrowed out of her owner's nearby enclosure. Video shared by Fife Signal Jammers shows Ava waddling along the roadside — nobody got sprayed. The owners set humane traps and are hoping she stays close to home. Scotland's wildlife officials say there's no public safety risk — but if Ava feels threatened, that's a very different story. She hasn't been caught yet. If you're driving near Kelty tonight, crack your window carefully. Will Ava find her way home before anyone gets sprayed?
